title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for pgx pooling, query contracts, transactions, and cancellation and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.
Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Pool Lifecycle · changed case · Quiz Feed Go Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 4830 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Sql Error Policy
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Go Sql Error Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go team handles case 9305 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove pgx pool, rows, one-connection transactions, cancellation, and error ownership; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify constraint, serialization, deadlock, cancellation, timeout, connection, and unknown failures without leaking SQL or secrets.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Sql Error Policy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Go Sql Error Policy, keep Feed Go Sql Error Policy connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
